subject: Anthropology
title: The Indus Valley Civilization
description: A fast breakdown of The Indus Valley Civilization for builders.

hook.text: Mohenjo-daro had flush toilets, covered drains, and a grid street plan — in 2500 BCE. Then it vanished.

buildup.text: The Indus Valley civilization spanned modern Pakistan and India with planned cities and standardized weights.

discovery.text: There are no palaces, no royal tombs, and no obvious kings. Its power structure remains an archaeological mystery.

twist.text: Its script has never been deciphered. Without reading their writing, we can't fully understand their world.

climax.text: The Indus Valley challenges assumptions. A complex civilization existed without obvious monarchy or military display.

punchline.text: A civilization that ran on order, not kings.

quiz.question: What is unusual about the Indus Valley civilization?
options: It had the largest army in history
It shows no clear evidence of kings or palaces
